allSecurityFindings.description: updateVport() calls requireUser() to confirm a session exists, but the UPDATE query filters only on .eq('id', vportId) with no ownership clause. Any authenticated user who knows a target vportId can overwrite another user's VPORT fields.

createBookingController accepts customerActorId as an independent caller-supplied parameter. The controller validates requestActorId but customer_actor_id is written as whatever customerActorId the caller passes — not enforced to equal requestActorId.
gradeSubmission controller performs direct Supabase table writes against learning.grades inside the controller layer, bypassing the DAL tier.
saveCourseMembership() and saveOrganizationMembership() perform raw Supabase writes directly inside the adminAccess controller file rather than a dedicated DAL.
markNotificationRecipientsSeenDAL filters by recipient_id but not by recipient_actor_id. If an attacker can enumerate recipient_id values, they can mark arbitrary notifications as seen.
UPDATABLE_COLS includes 'resource_id', 'service_id', and 'service_label_snapshot' — fields that could allow unauthorized rebooking manipulation if the controller's ownership check is bypassed.
dalMarkProfessionalBriefingsSeen reads/writes to vc.notifications (legacy schema) while the platform has migrated to notification.recipients + notification.inbox_items — creating dual-write drift.
VITE_LEARNING_DEBUG_REALMS and VITE_LEARNING_DEBUG_REALMS_BREAK are debug flags. If set to '1' in a production build, debug realm output could be rendered to end users.
